steps.title: Recognize the parallel lines shortcut
steps.content: When two lines are parallel, they have the same slope. Since line $k$ is parallel to line $j$, the slope of line $k$ equals the slope of line $j$. Now we just need to find the slope of line $j$.

steps.title: Find the slope of line j
steps.content:
Rewrite $6x - 7y = 14$ in slope-intercept form $y = mx + b$ by solving for $y$:
$$-7y = -6x + 14$$
$$y = \frac{6x - 14}{7} = \frac{6}{7}x - 2$$
The slope of line $j$ is the coefficient of $x$, which is $\frac{6}{7}$. Therefore, the slope of line $k$ is also $\frac{6}{7}$.
